topic: Songkran recap and upcoming hot-season cart schedule

turns.content: I was just looking at my sales notebook from Songkran and WOW na ka. I want to tell you what happened because you might remember I was stressing about it before 😂
Oh yes, Songkran! That was back in April — how did it go? Did you end up closing the cart for the 3 days like usual?

yes I close the 13th 14th 15th same as every year. but THIS year I did something smart na ka!! the days before Songkran — April 10 11 12 — I make family packs. 4 portions of khao man gai in one big box for 180 baht. I post on Instagram, take pre-orders through DM, put my sticker on every box. free marketing haha

That's a great strategy! Pre-orders mean you know exactly how much to prepare, and the sticker is clever branding going straight into people's homes. How many family packs did you sell over those three days?

first day only 12 packs because I post too late — only 2 days notice na ka. second day 22 packs because people share my post. and third day — the 12th — 31 PACKS!! I almost cannot finish cooking all of them. my friend Lek have to come help me pack the boxes 😂

31 packs at 180 baht each — that's 5,580 baht in family pack revenue alone on April 12th, on top of your regular plates! Over the three days that's roughly 11,700 baht from packs alone. Did that make up for the closure days?

not ALL of it but A LOT na ka. normally I lose about 25,000-30,000 baht from closing 3 days. the family packs plus extra regular plates from the busy pre-Songkran crowd — I make back maybe 18,000 extra. so I only really lose about 10,000 baht instead of 30,000. AND I got 3 days rest which I really need na ka
Cutting your losses by two-thirds is impressive, and you got actual rest instead of dodging water guns near hot charcoal. Are you planning to do family packs again next Songkran?

100 PERCENT na ka!! next year I will post 1 week early instead of 2 days. I think I can sell 40-50 packs per day if I plan properly. also I want to add a khao man gai dipping sauce bottle — like 50 baht each — so people can use at home with other food. upsell haha 🤔

The sauce bottle add-on is a smart upsell — low cost to produce, high perceived value, and it keeps your brand in their fridge for days after. You could even sell those year-round through your Instagram. But that's next April — what's happening with the cart right now in June?

ahhh June is the hard season na ka. rainy season start, fewer tourist walking around, and honestly it's SO HOT in the afternoon that even Thai people don't want to stand outside eating. my lunch rush get shorter — used to be 11am to 1:30pm, now everyone come between 11:30 and 12:30 only. one hour rush and then dead 💀
A compressed rush hour is tough — same prep work but a tighter window to sell everything. Are you adjusting your schedule or portions to account for the slower afternoons?

yes I cut my afternoon portion by about 30 percent na ka. instead of cooking 3 chickens for afternoon I only do 2. less waste that way. and I close earlier — 2pm instead of 3pm. no point standing in the rain with no customer haha. I use the extra time to do Instagram content and plan for August
Cutting portions to reduce waste is the right call when traffic drops. What's happening in August — Mother's Day promotions?

YES!! August 12 is Mother's Day in Thailand na ka. last year I didn't do anything special but this year I want to make a Mother's Day family pack — similar concept to Songkran but with a nicer box and maybe jasmine garland on top. Thai people always take mom out for food on that day. I think I can sell a lot if I plan early 🙏

You've basically built a playbook from Songkran that you can reuse for every holiday. Mother's Day family pack with a jasmine garland is a beautiful touch — very Thai, very thoughtful, and it makes the box feel like a gift instead of just takeaway food. Start posting teasers in late July and you'll have pre-orders locked in well before August 12th. Good luck with the rainy season in the meantime! 🙏
